identity.name: Educator Academy Module
identity.definition: An Educator Academy Module is a structured, self-paced professional learning unit that teaches educators how to use a specific feature or workflow in Renaissance Intelligence.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How an Educator Academy Module appears in the academy catalog

attributes.name: Module Title
attributes.description: Display name of the learning module.

attributes.name: Feature Area
attributes.description: Which product area this module covers: assessments, lessons, reports, classes, skills, or assignments.

attributes.name: Target Role
attributes.description: Who this module is designed for: teacher, admin, or both.

attributes.name: Format
attributes.description: Delivery format: video, interactive-walkthrough, step-by-step-guide, quiz, or blended.

attributes.name: Duration
attributes.description: Estimated time to complete in minutes.

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Current completion status: not-started, in-progress, or complete.

examples.title: Getting Started with Star Assessments
examples.description: Learn how to schedule, administer, and interpret Star Reading and Star Math assessments. This interactive walkthrough covers the full assessment lifecycle from scheduling to score interpretation.

attributes.name: Module Title
attributes.description: Display name of the learning module.

attributes.name: Description
attributes.description: Full description of what the module covers and learning outcomes.

attributes.name: Feature Area
attributes.description: Which product area this module covers.

attributes.name: Target Role
attributes.description: Who this module is designed for.

attributes.name: Format
attributes.description: Delivery format of the module.

attributes.name: Duration
attributes.description: Estimated time to complete in minutes.

attributes.name: Completion Criteria
attributes.description: What the user must do to mark the module complete: view-all, pass-quiz, or complete-practice.

attributes.name: Badge/Certification
attributes.description: Badge or certification name awarded upon completion.

attributes.name: Prerequisites
attributes.description: Other modules that must be completed before this one.

attributes.name: Content URL
attributes.description: URL to the module content.

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Current completion status.

attributes.name: Created Date
attributes.description: When the module was published.

attributes.name: Last Updated
attributes.description: When the module content was last revised.

stories.title: Browse Academy Catalog
stories.benefit: I can find training on the specific Renaissance feature I need help with and choose a format that fits my schedule.
stories.thenClause: I see available modules organized by feature area, with duration estimates and format indicators so I can choose what fits my available time.

stories.title: Complete Module and Earn Badge
stories.benefit: I have demonstrated competency with the feature and can reference my certification.
stories.whenClause: I finish all required activities in a module,
stories.thenClause: my status updates to complete, I receive a badge/certification if one is associated, and any linked onboarding steps are automatically marked complete.

businessRules.title: Self-Paced Only
businessRules.description: Modules are always self-paced. There are no scheduled sessions, live components, or cohort-based timelines.

businessRules.title: Prerequisite Enforcement
businessRules.description: If a module has prerequisites, the Start Module CTA is disabled until all prerequisite modules are marked complete.

businessRules.title: Completion Criteria Types
businessRules.description: Three completion modes exist: view-all (user must view all content pages), pass-quiz (score >= 80% on embedded quiz), complete-practice (user must complete hands-on practice activity).

businessRules.title: Progress Persistence
businessRules.description: Progress within a module is saved automatically. Users can leave and resume at the same point via the Resume Module CTA.

businessRules.title: Role-Based Filtering
businessRules.description: The academy catalog filters modules by the current user's role. Admins see all modules; teachers see teacher and both-targeted modules.

businessRules.title: Badge Award
businessRules.description: Badges are awarded automatically upon completion if a Badge/Certification value exists. Badges cannot be manually assigned or revoked.

businessRules.title: Content Versioning
businessRules.description: When module content is updated, users who previously completed the module retain their completion status. A 'New content available' indicator may appear.

businessRules.title: Onboarding Integration
businessRules.description: Completing a module that is linked to an Onboarding Step automatically updates the step's status to complete.

businessRules.title: Duration Accuracy
businessRules.description: Duration is an estimate provided by the content team. Actual completion time may vary. The system does not enforce time limits.

businessRules.title: No Deletion
businessRules.description: Modules cannot be deleted once published. They can be unpublished (hidden from catalog) but completion records persist.

states.name: Not Started
states.description: User has not begun the module
states.triggers: Default state when module is first visible to user

states.name: In Progress
states.description: User has started but not completed the module
states.triggers: User clicks Start Module or has partial progress saved

states.name: Complete
states.description: User has met all completion criteria
states.triggers: Completion criteria satisfied (view-all, pass-quiz, or complete-practice)

relationships.mechanics: An Educator Academy Module may be linked as the target of an Onboarding Step. Completing the module auto-completes the associated step.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A Module can be referenced by multiple Onboarding Steps across different checklists; each Step links to at most one Module.
relationships.sorts: Modules linked from steps: by feature area, by duration.
relationships.filters: By feature area, by target role, by completion status.
relationships.dependencies: If a module is unpublished, linked onboarding steps that reference it show a fallback help link instead.

relationships.mechanics: Teachers consume modules. The relationship tracks per-user progress, completion, and badge status.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Module has many teacher enrollments; a Teacher can enroll in many Modules.
relationships.sorts: Teachers within a module: by completion status, by last activity date.
relationships.filters: By completion status, by role (teacher vs. admin), by school.
relationships.dependencies: If a teacher account is deactivated, their completion records persist for reporting but they no longer appear in active enrollment counts.

allAttributes.name: Module Title
allAttributes.description: Display name of the learning module. Must be unique within the academy catalog.

allAttributes.name: Description
allAttributes.description: Full description of what the module covers, learning outcomes, and target audience.

allAttributes.name: Feature Area
allAttributes.description: Which product area this module covers: assessments, lessons, reports, classes, skills, or assignments.

allAttributes.name: Target Role
allAttributes.description: Who this module is designed for: teacher, admin, or both.

allAttributes.name: Format
allAttributes.description: Delivery format: video, interactive-walkthrough, step-by-step-guide, quiz, or blended.

allAttributes.name: Duration
allAttributes.description: Estimated time to complete in minutes.

allAttributes.name: Completion Criteria
allAttributes.description: What the user must do to complete: view-all, pass-quiz, or complete-practice.

allAttributes.name: Status
allAttributes.description: Per-user completion status: not-started, in-progress, or complete.

allAttributes.name: Prerequisites
allAttributes.description: Other modules that must be completed before starting this one.

allAttributes.name: Badge/Certification
allAttributes.description: Name of the badge or certification awarded upon module completion. Optional.

allAttributes.name: Content URL
allAttributes.description: URL pointing to the module's content delivery page.

allAttributes.name: Created Date
allAttributes.description: When the module was first published to the academy catalog.

allAttributes.name: Last Updated
allAttributes.description: When the module content was last revised or updated.

structure.evidence: An Educator Academy Module has rich attributes: title, description, feature area, target role, format, duration, completion criteria, status, prerequisites, badge, and content URL. You can design a detail page for a Module.

instances.evidence: 'Getting Started with Star Assessments' (15 min interactive walkthrough for teachers), 'Building Custom Reports' (25 min video for admins), 'Differentiated Assignments with Skills' (30 min blended for teachers). An academy catalog has dozens of Module instances.

purpose.evidence: Modules are the primary vehicle for educator professional development within Renaissance Intelligence. Teachers browse the academy, start modules, complete them to earn badges, and the system uses completion data to track onboarding progress and recommend next steps.

sipValidation.verdict: Domain content object. Structured PD delivery unit that drives educator activation and feature adoption.

synonyms.notes: Generic term; Module is more specific to the self-paced, structured format
Implies shorter, more focused content; Module is a broader container
Implies cohort-based or scheduled delivery; Module is always self-paced
Professional Development Module; used in admin-facing contexts

objectViews.description: Academy modules shown as catalog cards with feature area, role, format, and duration. Educators browse available training and start or resume modules.
The complete module record — description, prerequisites, completion criteria, and certification. Educators work through content, track progress, and earn badges from this page.
A module shown as a compact row within an onboarding checklist, linking professional development to the setup workflow.
